outcome_contract.why: Every reception screen should use a consistent, themeable visual language. Inline styles override the CSS cascade, raw buttons bypass DS interaction patterns, and unflagged layout classes make theming harder to iterate.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: All reception app components use DS Button for interactive elements, DS layout primitives where the ESLint rule flags violations, and contain zero inline style attributes. ESLint `ds/enforce-layout-primitives` warning count drops to 0.

engineering_coverage.applicable: Per-button variant selection preserves visual intent; DS Button provides consistent hover/focus states
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Visual regression risk is Low — DS Button is additive improvement

engineering_coverage.applicable: DS Button provides hover/focus/disabled states automatically; replaces manual className patterns

engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: No auth or data exposure changes

engineering_coverage.applicable: ESLint `ds/enforce-layout-primitives` → 0 warnings gate; TypeScript typecheck validates imports; snapshot updates in same commit
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: CI-only test run; snapshot updates needed
Purely presentational changes, no schema or API changes
CSS class changes only; no render path changes

stage_payload.summary: Three classes of DS violation remain in the reception app: 6 inline `style={{}}` attributes in 4 files, 35 raw `<button>` elements in 17 files (DraftReviewPanel already uses DS Button), and exactly 14 `ds/enforce-layout-primitives` ESLint warnings across 11 files (confirmed via live lint run on 2026-03-13 — 8 warnings in inbox/ScreenHeader/DraftReviewPanel files covered by TASK-02; 6 warnings in hub/StaffAccountsForm files covered by TASK-03). All changes are mechanical className/import replacements with no behaviour changes. Three disjoint task groups enable full Wave 1 parallel execution. Co
goals: Zero inline `style={{}}` attributes across all reception components.
All interactive elements use DS `Button` from `@acme/design-system/atoms`.
non_goals: Converting layout classes on elements not flagged by the ESLint rule.
Design or UX changes — purely mechanical compliance fixes.
selected_approach: What was chosen: Per-file mechanical conversion — read each button's current className → pick DS Button variant/tone; replace ESLint-flagged flex/grid with DS primitives; remove inline styles with Tailwind equivalents (keeping JS-computed dynamic values).

Why planning is not reopening option selection: Option B (wholesale rewrite) has higher blast radius; Option C (ESLint suppression) defeats the purpose; Option D (partial scope) contradicts user directive. Analysis verdict was decisive.

task_briefs.title: Fix all inline style attributes (4 files)
task_briefs.deliverable: code-change — remove all `style={{}}` attributes from 4 reception component files

task_briefs.title: Convert inbox + ScreenHeader DS violations (8 files)
task_briefs.deliverable: code-change — replace raw `<button>` elements with DS Button; replace ESLint-flagged flex/grid with DS layout primitives; resolve 2 arbitrary bracket values; in 8 files (6 inbox + ScreenHeader + DraftReviewPanel)

task_briefs.title: Convert remaining raw buttons and hub ESLint warnings (12 files)
task_briefs.deliverable: code-change — replace raw `<button>` elements with DS Button AND fix ESLint `ds/enforce-layout-primitives` warnings in 12 remaining reception component files

process_topology.note: None: no material process topology change — all changes are className/import replacements within component files.
